human_verification.notes: Verified child-care-specific records, source classes, retention and access boundaries, adjacent-lane routing, and scenario-to-ability coverage.

review_notes: All 12 scenarios pass with explicit refusal, escalation, and child-care-specific evidence behavior.
Functionality mapping covers all shared abilities, all records-documentation-and-administration abilities, and the cross-industry artifact-awareness ability.

specialist_owned_evidence: The pack names child-care-specific artifacts including enrollment, attendance, health, pickup, incident, staff, licensing, subsidy, and CACFP records.
Boundary handling is explicit for licensing interpretation, subsidy eligibility, privacy or custody disputes, and child-safety or clinical authority.
Retention, access, and public-record conflicts are escalated instead of overresolved.
Runtime remains `specification-based`, with no retained-implementation claim embedded in this package.
notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains; jurisdiction-specific questions are converted into required retrieval or escalation behavior.
Package is ready for validated spec-pack use and future retained-implementation promotion if repeated runtime demand justifies it.
